R01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Controller inhaler: yes - fluticasone (Flovent), prescribed daily, though she admits she skips it sometimes
profile.Rescue inhaler use: albuterol about 4 times a week, more during pollen season

R01.rationale: Diagnosed asthma, daily controller prescribed, rescue use 4x/week (above the 2x floor), no exclusions. Baseline easy case.

R02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Rescue inhaler use: exactly twice a week, 'like clockwork - Tuesday stairs and Saturday yard work'
profile.COPD or emphysema: never; his lung function tests have always come back 'asthma pattern, not COPD'
profile.Oral steroids: finished a prednisone course 5 weeks ago after a spring flare

R02.rationale: Triple boundary: age exactly 65 (inclusive), rescue use exactly 2x/week (inclusive floor of INC4), and prednisone finished 5 weeks ago (just outside the 4-week EXC3 window).

R03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Rescue inhaler use: maybe once a month - 'my asthma is honestly the best it's ever been'

R03.rationale: Rescue use about once a month - below the 2x/week floor. Her asthma is too WELL controlled for a study targeting uncontrolled asthma.

R04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.COPD or emphysema: ALSO diagnosed with COPD two years ago - his pulmonologist calls it 'asthma-COPD overlap'

R04.rationale: Carries a COPD (asthma-COPD overlap) diagnosis - a hard exclusion (EXC1) even though the asthma inclusion is met. The asthma-vs-COPD distinction is the test.

R05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Smoking: yes - about 8 cigarettes a day; 'I know, I know, with the asthma and everything'

R05.rationale: Current daily smoker (EXC2). Everything else qualifies.

R06.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Controller inhaler: NO - he has only ever been prescribed a rescue inhaler; 'the blue one, I use it when I need it'; no doctor has put him on a daily inhaler
profile.Rescue inhaler use: albuterol 5+ times a week, especially on cold mornings

R06.rationale: No daily controller inhaler prescribed (INC3) - only a rescue inhaler. Tests the controller-vs-reliever distinction; his heavy rescue use satisfies INC4 but that is not enough.

R07.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Asthma: diagnosed 30 years ago; a rough year
profile.Oral steroids: finished a prednisone burst 2 weeks ago
profile.Asthma hospital or ER visits: spent two nights in the hospital for an asthma flare 6 weeks ago

R07.rationale: Multi-failure: prednisone 2 weeks ago (inside the 4-week EXC3 window) AND asthma hospitalization 6 weeks ago (inside the 3-month EXC4 window). Both must be listed.

R08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Controller inhaler: UNKNOWN - 'I've got two inhalers, a blue one and a purple-ish one; the pharmacy just refills them. Which one I'm supposed to take every day versus when I need it, I honestly couldn't tell you - I use them when my chest gets tight'
profile.Rescue inhaler use: UNKNOWN weekly count - 'no idea how many times a week; bad weeks a lot, good weeks barely at all'

R08.rationale: Cannot identify which inhaler is a daily controller (INC3 unknown) and cannot estimate weekly rescue use (INC4 unknown) - the suite's only two-unknown case. Tests whether the bot lists BOTH unknown criteria.

R09.label: likely_eligible
R09.rationale: Everything qualifies (daily controller, rescue 3x/week). The test is conversational memory: producing an accurate mid-conversation read-back and still covering the remaining criteria.
